title: Mammillaria tenampensis
taxonomy: Mammillaria tenampensis is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is native to Mexico. The genus Mammillaria is part of the subfamily Mammilloideae, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti with fine, hair-like spines. Within the family Cactaceae, Mammillaria tenampensis is classified as belonging to the subfamily Mammilloideae. As a member of the subfamily Mammilloideae, Mammillaria tenampensis shares characteristics with other Mammillaria species.
uses: Mammillaria tenampensis is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Mammillaria tenampensis is also believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could be useful in treating infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.
